name: Roy Cooper
subject: This is who I am.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
I only need a minute or two.
Hello Peter,
I’m Roy Cooper, and before I ask for your contribution to our campaign for the U.S. Senate, I’d like to reintroduce myself and remind you why this fight is worth your attention— __and your investment.__
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98279?refcode=em20251128-9765&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9765%2E614464%2EuyY0qp)
I loved serving my home state of North Carolina as Governor, Attorney General, and a legislator before that. I’m a lucky husband and father. A diehard Carolina Hurricanes fan. A proud graduate of public schools. I’m the Governor who got NC’s Medicaid Expansion done and a fierce defender of your right to get an abortion if you choose to do so.
And I’m asking for your support to be the next Democratic Senator from North Carolina because this state is the tipping point — if we win here, we can take back the U.S. Senate from the GOP. But Republicans won’t make it easy on us. They’re aggressively targeting this seat as the key to their majority, and they’re willing to spend unprecedented sums against me. I can’t defeat them alone. Peter, can I count on you? **[If you’re with me, click here to help our team raise $825,000 online before the end of November.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98279?refcode=em20251128-9765&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9765%2E614464%2EuyY0qp)**
Look, I’m running for the U.S. Senate because I’m fed up with seeing Washington politicians strip away our rights, drive up the cost of living, tear apart our democracy and gut health care and SNAP benefits for folks in need.
I’m profoundly frustrated for women who have been prevented from accessing reproductive care; students whose special education services are on the line; and future generations who deserve leaders who will protect our planet, not contribute to its destruction.
**Peter, I’m ready to take on the national GOP, their powerful allies, and my well funded opponent — _with your help._ We have the momentum, but in a race this tight, every moment and every dollar counts. [Click here to split a donation between our campaign and Heartland Patriots and help close the gap to our November goal so we don’t lose the edge in this toss-up race.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98279?refcode=em20251128-9765&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9765%2E614464%2EuyY0qp)**
It is clear that we must achieve a balance of power in Washington to stop extreme Republicans from doing further harm to our nation. Flipping the Senate starts right here, in this toss-up race. That’s why national Republicans are already targeting me and directing huge sums to my far-right opponent.
Experts are saying this’ll be the most competitive Senate election of 2026 — and it might be the first-ever to cost over $1 billion dollars. _Talk about absurd._
**[I am asking you to step up by splitting a contribution between our campaign and Heartland Patriots before our end-of-month deadline because I can’t keep up with the GOP’s cash without your investment, and I can’t win this one without you on my team.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98279?refcode=em20251128-9765&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=9765%2E614464%2EuyY0qp)**
I will never stop fighting for this country, and I’m grateful to know you’re with me.
Roy Cooper

| [](https://www.facebook.com/lucaskuncemo/) | [](https://twitter.com/lucaskuncemo) | [](https://www.instagram.com/lucaskuncemo/)
---|---|---
Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce.com). Not authorized by any federal candidate or candidate committee.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you. But, if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.lucaskunce.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails_heartland-patriots/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.lucaskunce.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe_heartland_patriots/?t=5&akid=9765%2E614464%2EuyY0qp). Mail us: PO BOX 1240 Independence, MO 64050.

Use of military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform do not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

subject: NPR FIGHTS BACK against Trump >>>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
this is HUGE

subject: I’m missing YOUR NAME on my petition to demand voter ID laws!
body:

I’ve asked Conservatives all across the country to sign my petition to demand voter ID laws be enforced in all 50 states, but I haven’t seen YOUR NAME!
This issue is deeply important to me and it would mean the world to have your support on this. name: Team Sherrod
subject: this is what is at stake
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Able Dems PAC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
| | | Sherrod never planned to run for office again, Friend. But when he saw what was happening in our country – politicians in Washington siding with billionaires and corporate interests, costs rising for working families, health care being ripped away – he knew he couldn’t stay on the sidelines. There’s simply too much at stake for Ohio families. Our battleground race is going to be one of the most competitive in the country, and to win, Sherrod needs supporters like you to step up from the very start. **[Will you split $10 between Sherrod and AbleDems now to help us win in Ohio?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOSTD7SwWtKpEdVjlNw9fPTZjSYoLLMBkmEwapg-j86SEAkUjrG6p6_PdRapbpsH35L_yt43YtmnOxb1pHOuYkhf-cQdWyUS4FxAHaUQXWO1rYeCznkoYgH3-CbuHvgZYZq6Wc2xVAX_DZS6lmFTYdOAEfk4uSPyEd8n87gKXTj0CHeXADxOktmUYV-_9mMKv4VCo4LSFm-4YR8FFpqjIZR1qF4cora6E4dCiuDarJC0nIGO1RzNd3WaRBjNAiMwGO9IRqVZ_sx0I-6OIBpDPN1CQtsCSsFhOgtYRWKSKFIdL8R-KoUy1mEVkswjGI0Rjh/4lz/2URqhCLMQ9OWD7AF2MpS3g/h0/h001.Xpq0D7pwFtQdcXcw9PW9jZ2S_36JCvN1JXJ-Khq5i1M)**__ [Contribute](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOi-rtx1x3IjhiM4WMdAd9VE_jCVFrVCbvkDWl5AivfX8PARaiYbbkJx5_-EAeo-ZtYzyoxXn3A62iXUqNdJ6bBFbGhCPnY9cXfwXtjl6yzHu_ZllpoZIMcOWxEP7gQ0ioURoATy40IQQibsnUvo2JVUYbqKE_kuc1tO975i9h3KHAx3Q9W3HyuHN8on3qWaohiHNicse3OrhliAek4d99OHd_ESZwLUjEkwNLYAw_y1GtYF_2sweTiWKISscuEH1WkVgkysMIljWwNBo-_mablg/4lz/2URqhCLMQ9OWD7AF2MpS3g/h1/h001.6hONVPDg-aqpkeQohdmHJh9lSprmIo3vdJJY-JGMZBM) This race isn’t about left or right. It’s about whether Ohio workers have someone in the Senate fighting for them. Right now, no one is. Workers are more productive than ever, but wages are flat, costs are skyrocketing – and corporate special interests keep rigging the system.

Sherrod couldn’t stand by and watch. Not while regular people were getting hurt. He’s getting back in the ring because he believes in the Dignity of Work – the idea that hard work should pay off for everyone, no matter who you are or what kind of work you do. **[But he can’t do it without you, Friend: Will you chip in $10 split between AbleDems and Sherrod to win in Ohio and keep fighting for working clean_body: | | | Sherrod never planned to run for office again, Friend. But when he saw what was happening in our country – politicians in Washington siding with billionaires and corporate interests, costs rising for working families, health care being ripped away – he knew he couldn’t stay on the sidelines. There’s simply too much at stake for Ohio families. Our battleground race is going to be one of the most competitive in the country, and to win, Sherrod needs supporters like you to step up from the very start. This race isn’t about left or right. It’s about whether Ohio workers have someone in the Senate fighting for them. Right now, no one is. Workers are more productive than ever, but wages are flat, costs are skyrocketing – and corporate special interests keep rigging the system. Sherrod couldn’t stand by and watch. Not while regular people were getting hurt. He’s getting back in the ring because he believes in the Dignity of Work – the idea that hard work should pay off for everyone, no matter who you are or what kind of work you do. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. Thank you. – Team Sherrod elects Democrats with disabilities. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

A Tesla Explosion That Changes Everything

Near Trump Tower in Las Vegas, a Tesla Cybertruck exploded—and it’s raising serious questions.

The details are _shocking_. Gas canisters. Fireworks. A planned detonation.

But here’s the twist...

There’s something bigger behind this story—something most people haven’t noticed.

Do you see it?

[CLICK HERE to uncover the truth.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=69d43c538ffb49328c53844059026fec&_e=5H0fDOIpz3FtfDgPK43h5sORE2pOKlSGUOzbasmH-fSZbcIQF1IFsAOODEP3Qa2ZRKzR9jmU-L6FF37a-wjcRkznZ-U8QtrjjcDsPcgtrANkPPzS--EXBQWQGfwrsY88u7t4velCp9S8S1kT3JrIJo0QJaZacZt9xK16x4Eqd3GwhbN2uSeppJE9lzgstwZ-UUjEJVHC7jeAhbeVBxWkR0qDEhMPajXArnbXmD7LyDe7FZNJZ-LY_k_exBSZ8cth1LbQ443z8XadxuCXSY6kfCDt5jDxtSSt48oDc9gFMW0vvjLdJ4wtjOi0xP3iwQcFWv6EiMfSz-IV-bAovtxFDkXyJ3zJGz0zHbG8Nj2ia79Ipq6kLLJlSUqUYL3kh2bNoC4X5NCNtMig-j9-0uyKLPbyNiDVQP5kqEbINApB4XBNWzjCk2AUJ4kyRIUiYdbBQIwWbIwmJE9i71edxMC8wMt5YYfgoNOic4OgLTv9ukkP6VLWP3US3edu2ol_kmILFMl9Jw38ikYDnBTZqsWohIBKBH80KMBXO1_5nF70b80Nhx_9FEopRqwQPlnFbUkavhEaNoYEZ-ZPUthWj5ABPw%3D%3D)

You won’t believe what’s really going on.
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. body:
Boebert is weaker now than she has ever been. It’s time for us to strike.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001IeCTT3-pBqPihNEGQSMVuDiaQJtsLfRVLWwyTGdsihYd9WFP0RWcWDzTUPbmISq0zZ7O62BuT36KqG-OxgDB09UJCA3tL-xeotALeyv1GLzSi2qvYHDCyJOCuooOPHQsDG7E-_kSy4ho2IX6z1fl9I4JMG9qHYoWvg2_H2llyTyGW-I0bn0UxaFeEKhJVJ9ITTNM9Ltk7tpnmIpwFOl_--cFl8gBFN2C&c=wAGXOtjl9-KNhl_3KSbq0NpJHa095SQKy2BeQWK_a3ejrOEctesmxw==&ch=p-VcI82EshuVKf9AGEI80csTug0pvz0Fek4c3ywRrarybJtyKZ0QwA==)

Peter,
We are on the cusp of something incredible here, something that will change our country forever: defeating ultra-MAGA extremist Lauren Boebert.
Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024, I had the single best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts. Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every county, a sign of weakness for Boebert in this election when Trump will not be on the ballot.
But we can't afford to relax and let our guard down right now.
This district is my home. It's where I grew up. Where my parents lived, and where I cared for them when they passed away within days of each other after battles with cancer.
I know the people who also call this place home. People here are *angry* at Boebert for her role in destroying the economy, taking away healthcare, and ruining family businesses and farms, making it harder for the people who power and feed Colorado to do their jobs.
Boebert is weaker now than she has ever been. It's time for us to strike.
[Can I count on your support to defeat Lauren Boebert this election? If we work hard enough, Boebert could throw in the towel and resign early just like Marjorie Taylor Greene. Together, we can force Boebert into an early retirement:](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001IeCTT3-pBqPihNEGQSMVuDiaQJtsLfRVLWwyTGdsihYd9WFP0RWcWDzTUPbmISq0zZ7O62BuT36KqG-OxgDB09UJCA3tL-xeotALeyv1GLzSi2qvYHDCyJOCuooOPHQsDG7E-_kSy4ho2IX6z1fl9I4JMG9qHYoWvg2_H2llyTyGW-I0bn0UxaFeEKhJVJ9ITTNM9Ltk7tqifXZ9P0LjHnpm61dj7lLa&c=wAGXOtjl9-KNhl_3KSbq0NpJHa095SQKy2BeQWK_a3ejrOEctesmxw==&ch=p-VcI82EshuVKf9AGEI80csTug0pvz0Fek4c3ywRrarybJtyKZ0QwA==)

clean_body: Boebert is weaker now than she has ever been. It’s time for us to strike. | | | | | == | Peter, We are on the cusp of something incredible here, something that will change our country forever: defeating ultra-MAGA extremist Lauren Boebert. Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024, I had the single best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts. Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every county, a sign of weakness for Boebert in this election when Trump will not be on the ballot. But we can't afford to relax and let our guard down right now. This district is my home. It's where I grew up. Where my parents lived, and where I cared for them when they passed away within days of each other after battles with cancer. I know the people who also call this place home. People here are angry at Boebert for her role in destroying the economy, taking away healthcare, and ruining family businesses and farms, making it harder for the people who power and feed Colorado to do their jobs. Boebert is weaker now than she has ever been. It's time for us to strike. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | == Times like these require public servants who are willing to give everything they've got for the cause. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. body:
#
Hey Peter --
I know our campaign sends a lot of emails asking for money. So, I wanted to pause and explain why these asks are important.
Our political system is broken. Billionaire mega-donors and corporations can give unlimited money to try to buy elections.
It’s corruption. Plain and simple.
Those billionaire mega-donors will spend whatever it takes to beat us because they know that our campaign is a threat to their power.
So we have to raise the resources to take them on, and I refuse to take a dime from corporate PACs. Instead, I’m counting on the people to fuel this movement.
That’s where these emails come in. It may surprise you, but emails like this one are the number one way we raise money. Without the response to these messages, we’d stand no chance in Texas. So I’m asking:
[**Will you please consider making a donation to our campaign for the U.S. Senate? This is a people-powered movement. Your support is the backbone of what we’re building.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251128-1-ak&t=1&akid=269%2E1347274%2EV_b7ns)

name: INDEMS
subject: Kernel of Truth
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP03/1/67635/images/StandardLogo35.png]
Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party’s weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. If you were forwarded this newsletter, you can sign up to receive the Kernel every week by clicking here. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/G9OT47NWaEyN1002u8l4Tw2]

[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP03/1/67635/images/6021.png]

Happy Thanksgiving!

[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP03/1/67635/images/IDP%20Tday.png]

Redistricting Returns

[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP03/1/67635/images/Screenshot%202025-11-25%20at%203.07.48%E2%80%AFPM.png]

While the edistricting effort in Indiana appeared to be dead last week, the Republican supermajority announced this week that both the Indiana House and Senate will meet in December to begin drawing new gerrymandered congressional maps.

We’re kicking the fight into high gear to stop this extreme, unpopular maneuver. Trump and his out-of -state stooges have spent threatening and harassing Indiana legislators but Hoosiers are not giving up now.

Join us on Monday, December 8 at 9:30 a.m. ET at the Statehouse in Indianapolis for a rally to oppose redistricting.

https://www.mobilize.us/indems/event/870632/ [https://www.mobilize.us/indems/event/870632/]

https://www.mobilize.us/indems/event/870632/ [https://www.mobilize.us/indems/event/870632/]

Thousands of Hoosiers have already made their voices heard. Let's keep up the pressure and stop this once and for all.

Celebrate with IN Dems December 5

http://secure.actblue.com/donate/indemsholiday25 [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/indemsholiday25]

Join us on Friday, Dec. 5 for the Indiana Democratic Party’s annual Holiday Party in Downtown Indianapolis! This is our biggest party of the year, and we look forward to celebrating with you there.

While the Indiana GOP is divided and flailing, Democrats are more united than ever. Let’s finish 2025 off strong and get ready for a great 2026.

Get your tickets here! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/indemsholiday25]

Click here to see other opportunities and party events near you on Mobilize! [https://www.mobilize.us/indems/]

[data:image/png;base64,] [https://www.mobilize.us/indems/]

Information on County Party events, Town Halls, protests, volunteer opportunities, and more can be found on our Mobilize! [https://www.mobilize.us/indems/]

Get INDems Merch! [http://store.indems.org]

body:
The economy isn't working

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9oYWxleWZvcm1pLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.4xCZsfqii1Bt-IrjR_8WBOwmgce4zXRdf522OEVUvmg?) for more

# [What the Black Friday sales don't show](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6380051&post_id=179957223&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5OTU3MjIzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQzNDU5MDgsImV4cCI6MTc2NjkzNzkwOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTYzODAwNTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yAJLpyf5w9gDvrRDCsUsmpoOvosTVok0HPa5taQ9L3A)
### The economy isn't working
| | [Haley Stevens](https://substack.com/@haleyformi)
---
| Nov 28
---
| [](https://substack.com/@haleyformi)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack..JjIgPk3vAO2rSHbxhQ7wXggsR-x989iX18HbetQC3lw?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/haleyformi/p/what-the-black-friday-sales-dont?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Hi friends,
I hope you had a wonderful Thanksgiving. I hope you got some well-earned rest, spent time with your loved ones, and ate some delicious home-cooked food.
Today, many folks are plugging back in. Black Friday and this weekend are some of the biggest shopping days of the year - and one of the biggest tests of how people view the economy.
This year, things feel different. Yes, there are still plenty of sales, but the truth is, a lot of **people are anxious about affording even the basics** - like groceries and medicine.
Prices are still too high. Skyrocketing health care premiums are on everyone's mind. Families are cutting back. And working people all over Michigan are making hard choices about what they can afford - and what they can't.
**Wall Street might be doing fine, but it 's still incredibly hard to find a good job. And even when you do, prices are rising so dang fast that it feels impossible to get ahead. For too many, saving even a little money every month seems like a pipe dream.**
The truth is, a lot of Americans feel like the system just isn't working for them - and they're right.
While everyday people are stretching every dollar and praying they don't have a surprise emergency expense, billionaires are raking in record profits, and MAGA Republicans are using their power to cut food assistance, health care, and programs like Social Security - all while giving tax breaks to their ultra-wealthy donors.
Trump's chaotic tariffs and rising costs are hurting Michiganders - and it's time we put an end to them.
I'm running for Senate to fight for working families. Because I believe we can build an economy that actually works: one where families don't have to worry about paying massive medical bills, drowning in student debt, or affording necessities.
I'm in this fight because I've seen what's possible when we put people first. I know we can do it. For Michigan. For our country. For all of us.
Thank you for being in this with me.
With grit and gratitude,

Haley
* * *

A personal note from Haley 👋

I'm a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. I've spent my career fighting for Michigan's working families - and now I'm running for the U.S. Senate to keep standing up for the people I love.

But here's the truth: Senate control could come down to Michigan. And Trump and his megadonors know it. That's why they're gearing up to spend tens of millions to defeat me and hand this seat to a MAGA Republican.

I need your help to fight back. This race will be one of the most competitive - and expensive - in the country, and I'm counting on grassroots support to win.

If you're with me, will you chip in today?
[Donate](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/69c0fd0f-4185-48ee-9b84-fe5501cdf6c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
###### Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate

This race will be one of the most competitive - and expensive - in the country, and I'm counting on grassroots support to win. If you're with me, will you chip in today? body:
Boebert is weaker now than she has ever been. It’s time for us to strike.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Hp2PlvKnYhh82KLqDLqmwYNoKKkiOo8_BDy0gzissao7Qrxul6g-HP_TWlSPS10749LOf87DQyYoY4vKDWCURSaxvHQ7JaMFUv4JvYvIy6nkwLqBou34Y39iZYJKSIvmPxy4wdY6jAxqaZxO5dS8tqkSegcQHbQni0m1mMag2OkRQW1LNDos6EDqQcxJkrZe3aIVykQCD7wCKrWFQn1wWMSQa-z-EZNo&c=nLLZ7DuH9Ms5hFScHB3tQ62YSkQznPQ2zmXV_tqhWrb9WJsR_YFVGw==&ch=UIKQD9MXF930uiyhceG95vUPKTLrD3RxHhdox4ENethlfhZS1UPRKQ==)

|

Peter,
We are on the cusp of something incredible here, something that will change our country forever: defeating ultra-MAGA extremist Lauren Boebert.
Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024, I had the single best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts. Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every county, a sign of weakness for Boebert in this election when Trump will not be on the ballot.
But we can't afford to relax and let our guard down right now.
This district is my home. It's where I grew up. Where my parents lived, and where I cared for them when they passed away within days of each other after battles with cancer.
I know the people who also call this place home. People here are *angry* at Boebert for her role in destroying the economy, taking away healthcare, and ruining family businesses and farms, making it harder for the people who power and feed Colorado to do their jobs.
Boebert is weaker now than she has ever been. It's time for us to strike.
[Can I count on your support to defeat Lauren Boebert this election? If we work hard enough, Boebert could throw in the towel and resign early just like Marjorie Taylor Greene. Together, we can force Boebert into an early retirement:](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Hp2PlvKnYhh82KLqDLqmwYNoKKkiOo8_BDy0gzissao7Qrxul6g-HP_TWlSPS10749LOf87DQyYoY4vKDWCURSaxvHQ7JaMFUv4JvYvIy6nkwLqBou34Y39iZYJKSIvmPxy4wdY6jAxqaZxO5dS8tqkSegcQHbQni0m1mMag2OkRQW1LNDos6EDqQcxJkrZe3aIVykQCD7wvbFU0waVqNmpCJ_ZxCIEY&c=nLLZ7DuH9Ms5hFScHB3tQ62YSkQznPQ2zmXV_tqhWrb9WJsR_YFVGw==&ch=UIKQD9MXF930uiyhceG95vUPKTLrD3RxHhdox4ENethlfhZS1UPRKQ==)

Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024, I had the single best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts. Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every county, a sign of weakness for Boebert in this election when Trump will not be on the ballot. But we can't afford to relax and let our guard down right now. This district is my home. It's where I grew up. Where my parents lived, and where I cared for them when they passed away within days of each other after battles with cancer. I know the people who also call this place home. People here are angry at Boebert for her role in destroying the economy, taking away healthcare, and ruining family businesses and farms, making it harder for the people who power and feed Colorado to do their jobs. name: Voter Action Project
subject: 63% of Americans agree
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Pew Research Center: Majority of Americans continue to favor moving away from Electoral College [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?refcode=112825_1&amount=25]
As has been the case for over 200 years, the Electoral College determines the outcome of U.S. presidential races. Yet most Americans have long supported moving away from this system.
More than six-in-ten Americans (63%) would instead prefer to see the winner of the presidential election be the person who wins the most votes nationally. Roughly a third (35%) favor retaining the Electoral College system, according to a Pew Research Center survey of 9,720 adults conducted last fall.
We're clearly all tired of a system where all our candidates and their campaigns care about is whether the margin of victory is close enough in the state to make it worthwhile to campaign there instead of speaking to real Americans with real challenges.
The people who tell you we can't get rid of the Electoral College are wrong. Chip in $25 or more to help us change to a popular vote system once and for all >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?refcode=112825_1&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?express_lane=true&refcode=112825_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?express_lane=true&refcode=112825_1&amount=25]
GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?express_lane=true&refcode=112825_1&amount=50] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?express_lane=true&refcode=112825_1&amount=100]
GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?express_lane=true&refcode=112825_1&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?refcode=112825_1&amount=35]
Defenders of voting rights like Cory Booker, AOC, Robert Reich, and Stacey Abrams recognize that the Electoral College is harming our democracy and needs to go.
It’s the biggest tool the opponents of voting rights have to swing national elections and suppress the will of the people. We have to move to a system that's right and just over a system that benefits only themselves and silences others.
This is about the people's voices being heard and their votes meaning something . This is about a system that has rewarded the popular vote loser with the job anyway five times now. This about the people and the states that get ignored and lost along the way to *just enough* Electoral College votes.
The Electoral College is an antiquated system built for a vastly different America and we need to adapt to our changing needs as a democracy. Fuel our efforts with $25 or whatever you can today to help end it >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?refcode=112825_1&amount=25]
Thank you,
The Voter Action Project Electoral College Team
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/ [https://www.facebook.com/] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://instagram.com/ [https://instagram.com/] https://www.linkedin.com/ [https://www.linkedin.com/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=112825_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=112825_1&amount=20]

name: Pat Harrigan
subject: Protect Our Communities

body:
Stand with law enforcement

|
Friend,

**Charlotte is on high alert.** Federal officers are working tirelessly to enforce our immigration laws and protect our neighborhoods, and yet the Left is doing everything they can to stop them. | [ ](http://links.harriganfornc.com/u/click?_t=687026f8d8f7415aa539c5385c376b1f&_m=46c69ca11ce348afad5cba523cb27a35&_e=gNhaFrMf7NZ7xwszBIKTfAGy0XksMw1s0msB2IqSs0yLpW1eAbdFvGbl27yGIuibQTLkPd5Ny7LeA30lbFNLow8QUn3M02JGUmKKD0w7bcpkqsd_ZA2lN49Inj4spGpTelSoJd2GZJFga1Pa0cGxxRpBCgE0A1WtrOzv3qa1uF3_oMyGjG2xovBfPKHZD0_ZYReoAYVPe1b-N64-xZ26rGwJcIjQYIcM3Db5U8-INNVr4HFLgzKJvUBMVXoxaO3qgMxb1LoV8DyyKwKX4PIL36ulpqaBMZKtnMCcAyfP9lq_oO_Zt9_L2oq_HQ_wCHXKRrtNwmr2mdglaxUzVLi2E5hzFVJ1ZRGNYKep6t5X5168k-cMmgQgWUrdLyjNTqtcKMOnhKXbN0CpCnSqMwkYJq5JJNEn4HnMiyF-FF4Wzd_VdCPGG8ZliYLcZ3zkXbJOA_IBe9zkCe28lLGJtEATKMD1abVmZHDvzJTYu80_GXu-grOQltNL16_5jPxNcG-6Q1LUbgch-Em8riSHVS4PYQeEf4Jna6Hq_hhyam4vJxI%3D)
---
**Over 200 arrests have been made as part of Operation Charlotte 's Web, targeting individuals who have committed serious offenses.** Businesses, families, and communities are safer because of ICE's work, but Democrats are blocking, delaying, and undermining these efforts at every turn.
[**STAND WITH ICE NOW**](http://links.harriganfornc.com/u/click?_t=687026f8d8f7415aa539c5385c376b1f&_m=46c69ca11ce348afad5cba523cb27a35&_e=gNhaFrMf7NZ7xwszBIKTfAGy0XksMw1s0msB2IqSs0yLpW1eAbdFvGbl27yGIuibQTLkPd5Ny7LeA30lbFNLow8QUn3M02JGUmKKD0w7bcpkqsd_ZA2lN49Inj4spGpTelSoJd2GZJFga1Pa0cGxxdKuAOaSbkdZsr3EzHEMp5jv4MA_gqNb2vdehS570G2R-YxFPZxCDSOaYCDlGbKicMO2wn_picQjA6eL_iuWpI56vc2vNBPPdd4y1Qs8Rc8dAyo_T5kRBf4wbvdfJZB5GMRzhbIrHdJ5uVbp0w8zud1Bf_W018t-ZXBc7J_ckOP06PQRtewbFgERocI0IynkYOkWqwdnh37M3ymTAtXWJNjqdN95pDqAK6CQ4yLiIMizU3QAo1y5hSODaXJOR83p2bZb-JXLz2NH7RGj3dmu1pj1rbMaYLFq7Po7S_xmXwkPvDcZPs4s2FQxYtwhqL5kM-TEQL8i7b_HqBTktjvXW4aqgSp_poKaUOvxIo3dDzHFl5UhfAfbC1CouNHHexMrLs7gcVW9j0mTMK_oL6B9B4aMipkMFDpwmDj1wIOLO6La)

# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9246%2E5257348%2E9nni57)
Derek,
**[Is there anything we can say to get you to chip in? Adam has an end-of-month fundraising deadline coming up Sunday night, and he’s counting on you.](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q42025_abs_em.dd-KUe&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20251128_AM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=9246%2E5257348%2E9nni57)**
We’re hoping you’ll help us out.
So here it is, plain and simple:
What if we told you that Republicans shut down the government rather than extend the ACA tax credits, doubling or even tripling the cost of health care premiums?
Or what if we told you Trump is spending millions of dollars redecorating the White House while families were at risk of being denied SNAP benefits?
What if we reminded you that Congressional Republicans pushed through a brutal bill with major cuts to health care, food assistance, and renewable energy — just so billionaires could walk away with even bigger tax breaks?
**What if we told you that Adam Schiff is leading the charge to stop them and using every tool he can to defend our democracy and our country?**
And what if we told you he can’t do it without you?
**[Well, it’s all true, so if you’re able to pitch in today to support Adam’s work, it would mean a lot:](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q42025_abs_em.dd-KUe&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20251128_AM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=9246%2E5257348%2E9nni57)**

Peter –
There are only three days left of November, which means we only have three days left to reach our end-of-month fundraising goal. As we head into December, there is sure to be a slump in fundraising due to holidays, travel, and all the fun things that come with this time of year.
That’s why we’re asking you to show up for us now. Can you chip in any amount you can today to help us end November in the best way possible? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2511-q4-jg?refcode=251128-email]

name: Roy Cooper
subject: To whom much is given, much is required
body:
Grateful to have you with me in this fight. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120155116/585420527/1573338254?refcode=ea.90f091d8-75cc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.90f091d8-75cc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGYwOTFkOC03NWNjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ksFeD24IQMcEM7J2pIzDSVW8H2EXWI1PgGjDmlMe2HY=) Peter, when I left the Governor’s Office in January, it was with a great sense of humility and gratitude. Getting the chance to serve the state I love so much for eight years was an amazing opportunity. I thought that would likely be the end of my time in elected office, but it kept nagging at me that I might still be needed. **I saw how important this Senate seat was for not only our state, but for our country, and it got me thinking about a verse from the Book of Luke:** _To whom much is given, much is required._ I have received so many blessings in my life, Peter. Every race I’ve ever won; every opportunity I’ve ever been given — it was all possible because of people who helped me, supported me, and put their trust in me. **If there is still more I can do to give back to this country that I love, I have to try.** I know that we can win this toss-up seat. I wouldn’t be running otherwise. But I also know this will be the toughest fight of my career. The GOP is pouring funds into propping up my opponent, and polls are tightening. _I am asking for your help again._ **[I believe that there is so much left for us to accomplish together, and if we flip this seat blue and take back the Senate for the American people, we can start to get to work. Please, donate any amount you can before our November deadline on Sunday to stand with me.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120155117/585420528/1573338254?refcode=ea.90f091d8-75cc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.90f091d8-75cc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGYwOTFkOC03NWNjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ksFeD24IQMcEM7J2pIzDSVW8H2EXWI1PgGjDmlMe2HY=)** | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120155121/585420531/1573338254?refcode=ea.90f091d8-75cc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.90f091d8-75cc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGYwOTFkOC03NWNjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ksFeD24IQMcEM7J2pIzDSVW8H2EXWI1PgGjDmlMe2HY=)
---
Gratefully,
Roy Cooper
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120155126/585420536/1573338254?refcode=ea.90f091d8-75cc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.90f091d8-75cc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGYwOTFkOC03NWNjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ksFeD24IQMcEM7J2pIzDSVW8H2EXWI1PgGjDmlMe2HY=)
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

clean_body: Grateful to have you with me in this fight. | = Peter, when I left the Governor’s Office in January, it was with a great sense of humility and gratitude. Getting the chance to serve the state I love so much for eight years was an amazing opportunity. I thought that would likely be the end of my time in elected office, but it kept nagging at me that I might still be needed. I saw how important this Senate seat was for not only our state, but for our country, and it got me thinking about a verse from the Book of Luke: To whom much is given, much is required. I have received so many blessings in my life, Peter. Every race I’ve ever won; every opportunity I’ve ever been given — it was all possible because of people who helped me, supported me, and put their trust in me. If there is still more I can do to give back to this country that I love, I have to try. I know that we can win this toss-up seat. I wouldn’t be running otherwise. But I also know this will be the toughest fight of my career. The GOP is pouring funds into propping up my opponent, and polls are tightening. I am asking for your help again. = | = Gratefully, Roy Cooper = Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here. National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us. Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party. Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy. = = = mailto:info@roycooper.com | Cooper Victory Fund PO Box 1190 Raleigh, NC 27602 Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal. name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: President Trump is using the FBI to target me
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=5129%2E35263%2ET_HEa4)
Peter - This week, the FBI's Counterterrorism Division appeared to open an inquiry into me in response to a video the President did not like. In that video, I directly quoted the Uniform Code of Military Justice alongside five other Members of Congress with service and veteran backgrounds. Officers and enlisted in our military are trained to follow lawful orders, and refuse to follow illegal orders. It’s part of the training. This has been the case since World War II. Since then, the President has misquoted me, and then called for my arrest, trial, and death by hanging. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has threatened my friend, Senator Mark Kelly, with court martial. And now, it appears the president has directed the FBI to target the rest of us. **This is exactly why we made that video in the first place.** President Trump believes in weaponizing the federal government against his perceived enemies and does not believe laws apply to him or his Cabinet. He uses legal harassment as an intimidation tactic, to scare people out of speaking up. He’s done it to law firms, universities, private sector companies and private individuals. But these kinds of tactics won’t work on me. And neither will the total wave of death threats, bomb threats, and general harassment he has unleashed. I have always worked to be upfront and honest in these emails. This latest news from the FBI means that I have had to start thinking through a legal defense strategy — and we do not know exactly where President Trump and his Department of Justice will take this inquiry. And for the time being, all of that planning is being paid for through campaign dollars. **[So, your split contribution to Tammy Baldwin and me will be put to work immediately to ensure I have the resources to fight back.](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2155?c0=stb-em-slotkin.LRvF_a&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20251128_PM2_V1_S1&t=4&akid=5129%2E35263%2ET_HEa4)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Elissa Slotkin and Tammy Baldwin will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/3664?c0=stb-em-slotkin.mHp81-&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20251128_PM2_V1_S1&t=5&akid=5129%2E35263%2ET_HEa4)

To be frank, the whole thing isn’t about one line from one video. The events of the last week do not represent the country we know and love.
I refuse to believe that this is the new normal — where the President of the United States uses fear and intimidation against people with whom he disagrees.

clean_body: | Peter - This week, the FBI's Counterterrorism Division appeared to open an inquiry into me in response to a video the President did not like. In that video, I directly quoted the Uniform Code of Military Justice alongside five other Members of Congress with service and veteran backgrounds. Officers and enlisted in our military are trained to follow lawful orders, and refuse to follow illegal orders. It’s part of the training. This has been the case since World War II. Since then, the President has misquoted me, and then called for my arrest, trial, and death by hanging. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has threatened my friend, Senator Mark Kelly, with court martial. And now, it appears the president has directed the FBI to target the rest of us. This is exactly why we made that video in the first place. President Trump believes in weaponizing the federal government against his perceived enemies and does not believe laws apply to him or his Cabinet. He uses legal harassment as an intimidation tactic, to scare people out of speaking up. He’s done it to law firms, universities, private sector companies and private individuals. But these kinds of tactics won’t work on me. And neither will the total wave of death threats, bomb threats, and general harassment he has unleashed. I have always worked to be upfront and honest in these emails. This latest news from the FBI means that I have had to start thinking through a legal defense strategy — and we do not know exactly where President Trump and his Department of Justice will take this inquiry. And for the time being, all of that planning is being paid for through campaign dollars. name: Tim Walz
subject: Trump attacked me again last night
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
#
Peter,
While families across Minnesota and the rest of the country were celebrating Thanksgiving, Donald Trump used the moment to launch a barrage of attacks on Minnesota — using deeply offensive language and spreading lies about our immigrant community.
**Let me be clear: Trump is trying to distract us from the fact his administration is failing working people. It won’t work.**
We’re committed to building a Minnesota where people care for each other and everyone has a fair shot. The attacks will keep coming, but we’re going to stay steady — and we need to be ready.
I’m no stranger to Trump’s bullying, but there’s no way in hell that I’m going to sit back and take it.
This is just the beginning of what Trump and his allies have in store for us, and we need to make sure we have the resources to cut through the noise.
**[If you’re able, will you chip in $5 today to help us fight back and build the campaign we need to keep Donald Trump’s hateful agenda out of Minnesota?](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251128_3.X_kJAX&amount=5&refcode=em20251128-186&t=1&akid=186%2E34582%2EfsR63Q)**

Thank you for standing with us. It truly matters.
— Tim Walz

---|---|---

# [I Know What Big Oil's Thankful For: The Supreme Court's Bogus Ethics Code](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=180175961&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMTc1OTYxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQzNDc0ODcsImV4cCI6MTc2NjkzOTQ4NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.mmTKmSIg_SI1-RWAei9x3YZHhgbETuwYMNQoRPaKqR4)
### The fossil fuel industry wants SCOTUS to dismiss lawsuits about climate deception. Amy Coney Barrett and Samuel Alito both have major Big Oil conflicts of interest.

---
| Nov 28
---
| [](https://substack.com/@aaronregunberg)

---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=180175961&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMTc1OTYxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQzNDc0ODcsImV4cCI6MTc2NjkzOTQ4NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.mmTKmSIg_SI1-RWAei9x3YZHhgbETuwYMNQoRPaKqR4&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=180175961&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMTc1OTYxLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQzNDc0ODcsImV4cCI6MTc2NjkzOTQ4NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.mmTKmSIg_SI1-RWAei9x3YZHhgbETuwYMNQoRPaKqR4)

| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvYWFyb25yZWd1bmJlcmcvcC9pLWtub3ctd2hhdC1iaWctb2lscy10aGFua2Z1bC1mb3I_.HCsaTyYKL0WlM8PotI4-UzPZrLNg_8yPn48ivK4hnaE?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
